questions.q: Compare and contrast the social and economic conditions of the Rigvedic people with those of the Vedic period. How did the developments in the Vedic period influence the growth of Indian civilization?
questions.answer: The Rigvedic people were primarily pastoralists and their social and economic conditions were relatively simple. In contrast, the Vedic period saw the emergence of a more complex society with the growth of agriculture, trade, and commerce. The Vedic people were organized into a hierarchical system with the Brahmins at the top and the Shudras at the bottom. The developments in the Vedic period, such as the growth of agriculture and trade, influenced the growth of Indian civilization by laying the foundations for the emergence of more complex societies in the subsequent periods.
marking_scheme: Comparison of social and economic conditions of Rigvedic people and Vedic period (1 mark)
Contrast between social and economic conditions of Rigvedic people and Vedic period (1 mark)
Influence of Vedic period on the growth of Indian civilization (3 marks)
questions.topic: The Emergence of the Vedic Society

questions.q: Describe the geographical features of the Indus Valley Civilization. How did the geographical features influence the growth and development of the civilization?
questions.answer: The Indus Valley Civilization was located in the northwestern part of the Indian subcontinent, and its geographical features were characterized by a fertile alluvial plain, a well-developed system of rivers, and a hot and dry climate. The geographical features influenced the growth and development of the civilization by providing a fertile land for agriculture, a well-developed system of rivers for trade and commerce, and a hot and dry climate that allowed for the growth of crops such as wheat and barley.
marking_scheme: Geographical features of the Indus Valley Civilization (2 marks)
Influence of geographical features on the growth and development of the civilization (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the significance of the 'Great Bath' of Mohenjo-Daro in the context of the Indus Valley Civilization.
questions.answer: The 'Great Bath' of Mohenjo-Daro was a large public bath that was an important part of the Indus Valley Civilization. Its significance lies in the fact that it shows the high level of sophistication and urbanization of the civilization. The bath was constructed with advanced engineering skills and was an important part of the daily life of the people. It also reflects the importance of hygiene and cleanliness in the civilization.
marking_scheme: Importance of the 'Great Bath' in the context of the Indus Valley Civilization (3 marks)

questions.q: The Rigvedic people primarily relied on pastoralism, while the Vedic period saw the emergence of agriculture. Calculate the ratio of pastoralists to agriculturalists in the Vedic period, assuming that 60% of the population were pastoralists and 40% were agriculturalists.
questions.answer: Calculation: The ratio of pastoralists to agriculturalists in the Vedic period is 60:40 or 3:2.
questions.topic: The Emergence of the Vedic Society

questions.q: The Indus Valley Civilization had a well-developed system of weights and measures. Calculate the value of the copper weight 'Masha' in grams, assuming that 1 Masha is equivalent to 0.5 grams of copper.
questions.answer: Calculation: 1 Masha = 0.5 grams. Therefore, the value of 10 Mashe is 10 x 0.5 = 5 grams.

questions.q: The average yield of wheat in the Indus Valley Civilization was 1500 kg per hectare. Calculate the total yield of wheat in a farm of 10 hectares, assuming that the yield is uniform throughout the farm.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total yield = Average yield x Area = 1500 kg/hectare x 10 hectares = 15000 kg.
